tickets.title: US001: Student 1
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want to drag and drop courses into a calendar,
so that I can plan my semester visually.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Student can drag courses into the planner
- System saves the selected courses
- Schedule conflicts are highlighted

tickets.title: US002: Student 2
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want the system to warn me about missing prerequisites,
so that I can avoid invalid course selections.

Acceptance Criteria:
- System checks prerequisites automatically
- Warning message appears for invalid selections
- Students cannot confirm invalid schedules

tickets.title: US003: Student 3
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want to view course availability,
so that I know whether a class is full.

Acceptance Criteria:
- System displays available seats
- Full classes are marked clearly
- Data updates from the live database

analysis.ambiguity1: The statement "The course catalog is ugly" is vague because it does not specify what usability or design problems exist. There are no measurable UI requirements.
analysis.ambiguity2: The requirement "warn me if I’m choosing a class that starts at 8:00 AM" is vague because it does not specify how the warning should appear, when it should trigger, or whether students can ignore the warning and continue selecting the class.
analysis.conflictSolution: The system should allow students to add future courses as tentative plans, but prevent enrollment into currently full classes during actual registration periods.
